title: Race-grouped OOF stacking with sigmoid calibration only on held-out folds
summary: Two validation traps are likely here: same-race leakage through group-derived features and over-optimistic stacking/calibration fit on reused predictions.
rationale: Two validation traps are likely here: same-race leakage through group-derived features and over-optimistic stacking/calibration fit on reused predictions. GroupKFold and time-aware gaps are the safest official patterns for grouped temporal data. For ROC AUC, sigmoid calibration preserves ranking, while isotonic can change AUC via ties; that makes sigmoid the cleaner meta-feature transform.
implementation_hint: Use `GroupKFold` on `Year_Race` for all OOF generation; additionally test a forward-by-year split with a small gap around race boundaries for robustness checks. Train diverse base learners, generate OOF predictions, optionally apply fold-wise sigmoid calibration only on held-out data, then fit a simple logistic stacker on OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Less CV inflation and a more leaderboard-stable ensemble; often the gain comes from better blending rather than a stronger single model.
risk: Cleaner validation can lower local CV even when it improves public generalization; isotonic calibration is risky for AUC because it introduces ties.
